title: Central Bank Liquidity Swap Operations
gist: This is a NY Fed operational landing page for central bank liquidity swap operations, not a Liberty Street analytical post. The only genuine signal is the recent operations data table: a $49M U.S. dollar liquidity swap (ECB $48M, BoJ $1M) for the week settling 06/11/2026, flagged as small-value operational-readiness exercises rather than stress-driven draws.

insights.claim: As of the week settling 06/11/2026 the NY Fed's outstanding USD liquidity swaps totaled only $49M — $48M with the ECB and $1M with the Bank of Japan — and were flagged as small-value operational-readiness tests, indicating no offshore dollar funding stress.
insights.speaker: NY Fed
insights.so_what: Tiny, test-only swap usage signals calm offshore dollar funding; a jump to large non-test draws would be an early warning of global dollar-funding stress worth monitoring.
insights.quote: The New York Fed undertakes certain small value transactions from time to time for the purpose of testing operational readiness.

insights.claim: The standing dollar swap lines exist to let foreign central banks (BoE, BoJ, ECB, SNB) deliver U.S. dollar funding in their jurisdictions and to stop foreign money-market strains from spreading to U.S. markets.
insights.speaker: NY Fed
insights.so_what: The swap network is the Fed's first-line backstop against offshore dollar shortages; watch these results weekly as a global liquidity-stress gauge.
insights.quote: designed to improve liquidity conditions in global money markets and to minimize the risk that strains abroad could spread to U.S. markets
